biography: Louis Pasteur was a French chemist and microbiologist who founded the science of microbiology. He proved germ theory, developed pasteurization, and created vaccines for anthrax and rabies, fundamentally transforming medicine and public health.
aiSummary: Louis Pasteur was a microbiology pioneer who proved germ theory, invented pasteurization, and developed the rabies vaccine. Based in Paris, France.

timeline.title: Disproved spontaneous generation
timeline.description: Swan-neck flask experiments proved microorganisms cause decay

timeline.title: Developed pasteurization
timeline.description: Heat treatment process to kill harmful bacteria in beverages

timeline.title: Anthrax vaccine
timeline.description: Demonstrated successful vaccination of sheep against anthrax at Pouilly-le-Fort

timeline.title: Rabies vaccine
timeline.description: Successfully vaccinated Joseph Meister against rabies

timeline.title: Founded Pasteur Institute
timeline.description: Established the research institute that bears his name
